title: Real-Time Data with Tavily
tagline: Add Tavily fresh web context to a Pinecone-backed book recommender so stale vectors can answer current questions.

story.problem: Cookbook #2 can recommend from a Pinecone-backed Goodreads corpus, but that corpus stops around 2017 and cannot carry constantly changing pricing, availability, editions, or newly released books.
story.solution: Keep Pinecone for curated book memory, then add Tavily — a Nebius partner — for live web grounding before the Nebius answer model writes the final recommendation.
story.outcome: A streaming book recommender that combines static semantic retrieval with fresh web context, cites both retrieved books and live sources, and keeps time, tokens, and cost visible.

prerequisites: A Pinecone API key and the Goodreads book vectors from Cookbook #2

quickstart.configure: cp .env.example .env  # then fill NEBIUS_API_KEY, PINECONE_API_KEY, and TAVILY_API_KEY

endpoints.description: Recommend books from Pinecone, augment with Tavily freshness, and stream SSE events back.
